name: Sweet and Sour Cabbage (Emeril)
recipeIngredient: 1 Granny Smith Apple, cored and diced
6 ounces roasted pork loin, sliced into 1-inch slices
1/2 cup shallot reduction (veal reduction with julienned shallot)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large non-reactive sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
2. When the oil is hot, add the bacon and cook until crispy, about 3-5 minutes. Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon and drain on a paper-lined plate.
3. Sauté the onions in the bacon fat for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are softened but not browned.
4. Add the cabbage, season with salt and pepper, and sauté for another 2-3 minutes.
5. Stir in the apples, vinegar, bay leaves, cinnamon sticks, allspice, and water. Bring to a simmer and cook for 3 minutes.
6. Stir in the sugar until it dissolves completely, about 2 minutes.
7. Cover the pot and continue cooking over medium heat for 15 minutes.
8. Turn off the heat and uncover the pan. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until all moisture has evaporated, about another 15 minutes.
9. Stir in the bacon pieces and parsley. Taste and adjust seasonings if needed.
10. Plate a slice of pork loin in the center of each serving plate. Alternate layering the cabbage and pork slices on top until all the pork is used.
11. Scatter the fried leeks over the cabbage, then drizzle the shallot reduction around the plate.
12. Garnish with chives and brunoise peppers before serving.
Yield: about 2 cups
